docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:669:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1405:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:670:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1800:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:671:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2416:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:672:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2446:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:673:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2450:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:674:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2696:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:682:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3259:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:683:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3262:docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:684:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3263:docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:685:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3264:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:686:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3265:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:687:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3266:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:688:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3267:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:689:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5557:   138	- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:727:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:728:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1429:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:729:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1433:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:730:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:731:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1442:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:732:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

File sizes are compliant: all new files are under 300 LOC, largest is `tests.md` at 273. The planned audit test still has weak spots: [tests.md:34](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:34) depends on non-existent `AppActionMeta`, and [tests.md:213](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:213) does not actually assert lane distinctness.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:738:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1508:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:745:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1607:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:746:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1609:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2404:++- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:771:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:772:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1649:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:773:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1650:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:794:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1692:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:795:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1693:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:796:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1694:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:797:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1695:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:852:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:853:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2071:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:891:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1838:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:905:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:12:- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:19:- Either way: this is at least one file (~200 LOC) of NMP-specific glue per platform — AND the framework-magic contract (`docs/design/framework-magic.md`) must promise the app dispatches zero code while the kernel does the stop/restart on kind:3 arrival.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:32:### 3. No persisted outbox tracker
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:34:`OutboxTracker.data` is an in-memory LRU (`core/src/outbox/tracker.ts:62-65`). On every cold app start, NDK re-fetches relay lists for every author the user cares about. For mobile NMP, this is a cold-start cost ~= 1 round-trip to outbox relays per ~400 authors.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:78:`NDKSync.sync` / `syncAndSubscribe` are not the default path. Regular `ndk.subscribe` doesn't try negentropy. For large historical fetches (e.g., loading the last week of a 500-follow feed cold), NMP should reach for sync directly.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:86:When the outbox model picks 2 relays per author, those 2 may both be slow. No automatic fallback or expansion mid-stream. NMP could add: monitor per-relay event-arrival rate; if below threshold for a given author, expand to N=3.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:7:### A1. Why snapshots + ViewBatch (and not other patterns)
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:9:The bible mandates snapshots over FFI. For a Nostr client with timelines of thousands of events, naive full-snapshots are wasteful. We deviate as follows:
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:27:Three serious alternatives to snapshots+ViewBatch were evaluated. Each is used in production by other apps. Each was rejected (or deferred) for specific reasons.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:43:This is a v2 decision gated on Phase 9 data. v1 ships with snapshots+ViewBatch only.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:66:| 17 | Private DMs | §7.10 |
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:74:| 47 | Wallet Connect | §7.9 |
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:81:| 65 | Relay-list metadata (outbox) | §7.3 |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:15:You are reviewing merge 8302cc1 (docs(m11): fix second codex pass — split wording, proof cmd, route env var) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This applies second-pass codex fixes on M11 podcast docs. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:18:- D2: negentropy first
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:19:- D3: outbox routing automatic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what is open

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:112: - `podcast.llm.local_base_url`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:115:+**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:120:Verify fixes land cleanly, no new TODO/FIXME, no file exceeds size budget, no new doctrine issue. Be terse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:122:I’ll verify the merge against the live checkout rather than the pasted diff: commit presence, touched-file content, TODO/FIXME scan, LOC budget, and doctrine consistency in the edited docs.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:411:+**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:422: - Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:426: The rig.rs route through `rig-core` is **required to land and pass its end-to-end test in M11**. It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:465:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:466:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:284:The rig.rs route through `rig-core` is **required to land and pass its end-to-end test in M11**. It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:467:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:68:- Special: each `Toggle($settings.allowCellularDownloads)` becomes a `.onChange` that calls `useSettings().dispatch(.updateSettings(patch:))` — keeps the SwiftUI binding shape while making the kernel the source of truth.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:468:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:79:4. `QueueView.swift` — `useProcessingQueue()`. Reads the kernel ledger.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:469:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:88:- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:470:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:113:- Acceptance: play an episode → MiniPlayer appears → tap → PlayerSheet opens → seek works → background-audio survives lock screen; remote control center play/pause/skip all route through the kernel ledger; ad-skip behavior matches reference.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:471:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:145:- Categories section → `useDiscoverCategories()` reading the static list — no kernel call.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:472:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:216:- [ ] No `nmp-core` patches in this PR (kernel must stay app-agnostic)

Per-job statuses are kernel ledger rows. | `podcast-core` orchestrator | all of the above |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:474:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:56:| `Services/ServiceContainer.swift` | 54 | Deleted. ServiceContainer is the kernel actor. | — | n/a |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:476:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:119:| `Views/Library/QueueView.swift` | 129 | `useProcessingQueue()` | `ProcessingQueueViewModule { active: [QueueJobRow], completed: [...], failed: [...] }` (reads directly from the kernel action ledger) | `Ledger::CancelAction`, `Ledger::ClearCompleted`, `Ledger::ClearFailed` |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:481:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:27:| Subscribe to 10 real podcasts | `docs/perf/m11/subscriptions.md` with the 10 feed URLs + parsed metadata snapshot | Scripted: `just demo-subscribe-10` runs `SubscribePodcast` actions and dumps `PodcastRecord` JSON |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:482:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:486:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:487:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:256:| `ProcessingQueueViewModule` | `ProcessingQueueSpec {}` | `{ active, completed, failed }` (each is `Vec<QueueJobPayload>`) | the kernel action ledger — selector: `namespace LIKE 'podcast.*'` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:488:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:320:// Updates: NowPlaying view module gets a snapshot; clears lastSkippedAdChapter.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:489:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:378:### D.8 Atomicity contract (per [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §4)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:490:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:384:## E. Projection caches (kernel-owned, module-defined)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:491:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:392:Per ADR-0001, projection cache changes feed `ProjectionChange` events into `ViewModule::on_projection_changed`. Each is registered as a `ProjectionCache` in `nmp-core::substrate::projections` (kernel-owned trait; modules opt in).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:492:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:407:kernel = "nmp-core"
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:493:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:421:    case kernel(KernelAction)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:494:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:36:  2. Golden tests: same input → both shapes → snapshotted; reviewed when changed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:495:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:51:- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:496:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:101:- **Recommendation**: (a) with snapshot writes. Future ADR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:497:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:122:- **Context**: A short transient `AVPlayer` plays a recorded thought when the user taps "Play recording" on an InsightCard. Currently planned as Bridge-only (UI plays it, kernel doesn't know).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:498:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:244:- **Allowed drift category B: dynamic data** — screens that include `RelativeDateTimeFormatter` output ("2 min ago") are pinned to a fixed `Date()` via `simctl status_bar override` + a `--frozen-time` flag in the rebuild that the kernel reads as `now_ms_override`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:499:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:297:- **Reference repo divergence**: the canonical Swift app may be edited after Step 0. The screenshot harness pins to a recorded `cp -R` snapshot under `ios/NmpPodcast/.reference-snapshot/` (gitignored) for re-capture; the snapshot points at a SHA in `../podcast` written into `parity-screenshots.md` so re-baselining is deliberate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:500:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:501:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:6:M11 adds **nine** new `CapabilityModule`s to the kernel's reusable set. Each is generic (no podcast nouns in request or result types) so a future Pika-like, Highlighter, or messaging app could reuse them. Each is **idempotent** (start/stop/restart safe per RMP bible commandment #7) and has **bounded native state** (the bridge holds only the OS handle; no caches, no derived state, no policy).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:502:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:40:    Tick { position_s: f64 },                      // ≤ 4 Hz from bridge (kernel coalesces)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:503:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79:- `Load { url_a } → Load { url_a }` → the bridge executes every `Load` unconditionally: it tears down the current item (KVO removed, observer removed, `AVPlayer` released) and sets up a fresh `AVPlayer`. Coalescing duplicate-URL loads is the **Rust kernel's responsibility** (`podcast-core::player` ActionModule checks whether the requested episode is already loaded before dispatching `AudioRequest::Load`; the bridge never short-circuits on its own).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:504:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:85:### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:505:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:109:iOS impl: `BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(forTaskWithIdentifier:)` per `RegisterTask`. The bridge fans out wake events to the kernel; the kernel dispatches the appropriate action (e.g., `Podcast::RefreshAllFeeds` on `AppRefresh`, drains pending downloads on `Processing`). Idempotent: re-registering with the same identifier replaces the prior schedule (`BGTaskScheduler.submit`). Bounded state: BGTaskScheduler is OS-owned; the bridge holds only the identifier→completion-handler map for currently-running tasks.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:506:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:189:The kernel-owned policy: which model to use (constant in M11), how to chunk overflow text (truncate at 256 tokens; documented as the bge-small ceiling), retry policy (zero retries on `Unavailable`, two retries on `Error`). The bridge reports facts; Rust decides.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:507:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:82:Their target app has Wiki, Briefings, CarPlay, Watch, NIP-46, NIP-44, Cashu, peer-agent tool gates, App Intents/Siri, Live Activities — none of which exist in `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast`. Including them in M11 would defeat the kernel-boundary proof (the kernel-boundary check is only meaningful when the app stays small enough that the kernel could conceivably grow podcast nouns to make it easier).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:508:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:90:That's their roadmap for their bigger app. Ours is M11 — one milestone — because we're proving a single architectural claim (the kernel boundary), not shipping a v1 product.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:509:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:106:- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:510:docs/design/podcast/screenshots.md:47:- **Snapshot Testing in XCTest** (`pointfreeco/swift-snapshot-testing`) — tied to the candidate app's test target, which adds build-graph weight; cannot compare two apps.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:511:docs/design/podcast/screenshots.md:48:- **Apple's `xcrun simctl ui appearance`** — diff tools sit at the snapshot level, not the simctl level.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:512:docs/design/podcast/screenshots.md:126:- **Candidate loader** (Rust): a hidden `kernel.test.SeedFixture { json }` action wired only behind a `#[cfg(feature = "test-seeds")]` build of `podcast-core`. Same JSON.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:513:docs/design/podcast/screenshots.md:205:- In-flight animations (we only snapshot steady state; the DSL waits for the relevant accessibility label).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:514:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:7:`podcast-feeds` owns feed fetching + parsing + Podcast Index API integration. It is the only crate that speaks RSS/Atom/JSON Feed/Podcasting 2.0; everyone else consumes parsed records over the kernel boundary. It has **no view modules** — discovered data is materialised by callers (`podcast-core::DiscoverViewModule`, `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast` action).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:515:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:66:Conditional GET (ETag / If-Modified-Since) for `RefreshFeed`: the kernel persists a `FeedFetchCache { feed_url, etag?, last_modified?, fetched_at_ms }` row per podcast. On refresh: send the headers; on 304 → emit `RefreshFeedOutput::NotModified`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:516:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:517:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:230:        each token: kernel → AskAction::reduce(AssistantTokenArrived)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:518:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:235:        kernel → AskAction::reduce(StreamFinished)

It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:593:The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads these vars at boot and writes them into the `podcast.llm.*` keys; the bootstrap also overwrites `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }`, so `select_route()` picks the rig path without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:621:> Parent: [`../podcast-app-rebuild.md`](../podcast-app-rebuild.md) · sibling: [`inventory.md`](inventory.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:628:Two files in `../podcast/PodcastApp/Views/` exceed the 500 LOC hard limit (`DiscoverView.swift` 898 LOC; `PlayerSheet.swift` 642 LOC). Both exceed the 300 LOC soft limit. **The right time to split them is during the copy step** — we split and copy atomically in one commit, not as a separate refactor PR. The rendered UI is unchanged (same `View` bodies, same `@ViewBuilder` sections); only the file boundaries move.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:677:    diff <(grep -v '^import\|^$' ../podcast/PodcastApp/Views/Library/DiscoverView.swift) \

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:705:This is the same lesson the podcast-rmp project learned the slow way (cf. their `docs/plans/iphone-view-fidelity-restoration.md` — restoring fidelity *after* drift was a multi-week effort). We pay the upfront cost.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:793:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:7:`podcast-core` is the **central app crate**. It owns every domain record (Podcast/Episode/Transcript/Chapter/Guest/Insight/PlayerState/QueueEntry/Activity/Subscription/SettingsRecord) and every non-LLM, non-RAG, non-feed-parsing view + action module. It depends on capabilities defined in [`capabilities.md`](capabilities.md) but does not implement them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:794:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:262:| `SettingsViewModule` | `SettingsSpec {}` | `{ settings: SettingsRecord, version, build }` | settings record |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:795:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:14:For Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) the same actions route through `rig.rs` against a configured provider. The action shape is identical; the routing decision lives in `podcast-llm/src/router.rs` and reads from `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:796:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:67:pub fn select_route(settings: &SettingsRecord, capabilities: &CapabilityAvailability) -> LlmRoute {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:797:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:68:    match settings.llm_preferred_route {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:798:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:264:- `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` — `openai` | `anthropic` | `local`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:799:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:266:- `NMP_LLM_API_KEY` — provider API key (OpenAI/Anthropic)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:800:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:267:- `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL` — required only for `local`; populates `RigProvider::Local { base_url }`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:801:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:269:The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads these vars at boot and writes them into the `podcast.llm.*` keys; the bootstrap also overwrites `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }`, so `select_route()` picks the rig path without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:802:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:293:Apple Intelligence remains the default iOS route. The rig.rs path is the non-Apple route that M11 must demonstrate. Both routes share the same action shape — the only difference is `select_route()`'s return value.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:803:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:67:- Capability deps: `KeyValueStoreCapability` (lives behind `useSettings`; SettingsRecord is read-through).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:804:./docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:7:`podcast-core` is the **central app crate**. It owns every domain record (Podcast/Episode/Transcript/Chapter/Guest/Insight/PlayerState/QueueEntry/Activity/Subscription/SettingsRecord) and every non-LLM, non-RAG, non-feed-parsing view + action module. It depends on capabilities defined in [`capabilities.md`](capabilities.md) but does not implement them.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:811:./docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:264:- `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` — `openai` | `anthropic` | `local`

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:814:./docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:269:The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads these vars at boot and writes them into the `podcast.llm.*` keys; the bootstrap also overwrites `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }`, so `select_route()` picks the rig path without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:815:./docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:293:Apple Intelligence remains the default iOS route. The rig.rs path is the non-Apple route that M11 must demonstrate. Both routes share the same action shape — the only difference is `select_route()`'s return value.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:816:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:213:The reason this is a capability (not Rust-owned storage): the reference Swift app uses `UserDefaults`; we keep that surface so iOS user data migrates seamlessly. **Authoritative settings live in Rust** (`SettingsRecord` is the source of truth); UserDefaults is just the platform persistence backing.

docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:76:`AppState` does **not** include the entire event store contents. Events are reached through `ViewSnapshots` which carry only the events relevant to currently-open views. This is the v1 resolution of `aim.md` §7.1: full state snapshots, but the "state" is a projection of open views — bounded by what the UI is showing.

docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:162:- A `tag()` method (`pub fn tag(&self) -> &'static str`) returns a log-safe label that never reveals secrets (mnemonics, nsec, plaintext DMs).
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:166:`AppUpdate` is the outbound stream. Bible doctrine: snapshots by default; granular variants only where profiling warrants. v1 starts with:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:17:M2 goal (per docs/plan.md §M2): replace hardcoded relay set with a subscription compiler stage. Logical interests compile to per-relay plans. NIP-65 outbox routing default for reads + writes. Four diagnostic lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) never collapse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:28: docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md     | 224 +++++++++++++++++
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:34:031fc07 design(m2): subscription compilation + outbox routing spec
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:37:driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, plus an outbox-routing planner subsystem
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:62:  - subscription-compilation/outbox.md    PublishPlanner trait + default
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:96:+> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:105:+- [Outbox — publish-planner seam + override action](subscription-compilation/outbox.md) (§7)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:118:+| 7 | `PublishPlanner` trait, write fan-out policy, override + debug warning | outbox.md |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:130:+4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:133:+7. **NSE crate compilation surface.** `nmp-nip17-nse` (M9) runs in iOS Notification Service Extension with bounded memory; it needs to compile a single-author single-relay plan without the full planner. Confirm in an ADR that the compiler exposes a `compile_one(spec, mailbox_cache_snapshot) -> Plan` pure function suitable for NSE use, and that the function does not require a live actor.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:219:+`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:526:+Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per `docs/plan.md` line 139).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:536:+> Read first: `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7 (subscription compilation lessons), §2 (NDK outbox lessons), §9 (NMP principles).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:638:+`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:735:+    type Delta   = MailboxesPayload;       // payloads are small; emit whole snapshots
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:825:+pub fn resolve_author_outbox(
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:856:+- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:869:+- `nmp-core::kernel::planner` — for `MailboxCache`, `resolve_author_outbox/inbox`, `parse_relay_list`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:871:+- Future `nmp-nip17` (M9) — DM publish path imports `resolve_author_inbox` for recipient lookups.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:896:+++ b/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:901:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:905:+This is the framing the parent index calls out as "design seam now, first concrete consumer in M6." Without this seam, M6 risks reinventing outbox routing inline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:946:+    Author,        // this relay is in the plan as the author's outbox
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1004:+     author_outbox = resolve_author_outbox(cache, user_configured, indexer, event.pubkey)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1005:+     If author_outbox.relays is empty:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1009:+        assignments = [each author_outbox.relays → AuthorWriteRelay { lane }]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1017:+        // intentionally NO author-write inclusion: private events do not go to public outbox
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1020:+        assignments = author_outbox ∪ union(each notify pubkey's inbox)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1021:+        required_success_count = max(1, ceil(author_outbox.len() / 3))
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1268:+M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1338:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1982:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1339:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2002:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1340:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1343:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2099:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:59:│  - no outbox routing yet (hardcoded relay)                   │
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1344:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2309:   186	- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1345:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2350:     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1346:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2354:     8	This is the framing the parent index calls out as "design seam now, first concrete consumer in M6." Without this seam, M6 risks reinventing outbox routing inline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1347:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1348:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3411:     6	> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1349:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1350:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3556:   106	`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1351:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1357:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1358:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1359:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1363:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1367:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1368:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1369:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1370:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1398:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1399:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1400:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1401:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1402:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1403:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1404:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1405:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1406:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:7:Applesauce's outbox is not a relay-resolver service. It is a **reactive composition of three RxJS operators** over the EventStore. There is no central "outbox service" — there is just `OutboxModel = contacts() ⇒ includeMailboxes() ⇒ selectOptimalRelays()`. Every consumer that wants outbox routing subscribes to the model directly.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1407:docs/plan/m10-blossom.md:23:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1408:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:15:You are reviewing merge fb139ab on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. File-size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1410:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:21:4. S10 conditional vs doctrine — S10 should no longer be used as D1 doctrine evidence; D1 should cite S3+S4+code proof
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:59:+You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1412:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:61:+M2 goal (per docs/plan.md §M2): replace hardcoded relay set with a subscription compiler stage. Logical interests compile to per-relay plans. NIP-65 outbox routing default for reads + writes. Four diagnostic lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) never collapse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1413:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:78:+031fc07 design(m2): subscription compilation + outbox routing spec
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:140:++> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1415:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:591:++- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1416:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:682:++`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1417:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:900:++- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1418:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:945:++> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1419:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:949:++This is the framing the parent index calls out as "design seam now, first concrete consumer in M6." Without this seam, M6 risks reinventing outbox routing inline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1420:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1156:++- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1611:+Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1428:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1742:+/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|Doctrine|kernel never grows|single writer|capabilities report|M2|subscription compiler|subscription compilation|outbox|NIP-65|watermark|LMDB|relay-set|relay set" docs/plan.md docs/aim.md docs/product-spec/subsystems.md docs/design/kernel-substrate.md docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md docs/design/reactivity/*.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1429:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1748:+docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:9:NDK and Applesauce both solve real Nostr client problems that NMP must eventually solve: relay discovery, outbox routing, cache-backed reads, action-driven writes, derived models, and sane application defaults. They do it with different API philosophies and runtime assumptions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1430:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1749:+docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:17:NDK's strongest lesson is that outbox routing should feel automatic to application developers. A developer should not normally decide which relays to use for a profile, timeline, reply, or publish. The framework should infer the right relay set from NIP-65 metadata, event tags, relay hints, and fallback policy.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1431:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1756:+docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:103:This also keeps performance policy centralized. The same planner that owns outbox routing can enforce limits, deduplicate page loads, avoid duplicate boundary events, and prevent multiple components from opening equivalent pagination work.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1432:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1759:+docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:153:- From NDK, take the ambition that outbox routing is automatic by default.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1433:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1765:+docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:173:- Pre-formatted display fields per doctrine D1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1434:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1768:+docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:452:- `nmp-nip65`: Mailboxes view module + outbox routing helper.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1435:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1771:+docs/aim.md:89:These crates are **dependencies, not forks**. The framework's job is to compose them into an opinionated whole; their authors do the protocol correctness work, we do the application-layer work. Where they have gaps relative to the framework's goals (reactive queries, models, sessions, web-of-trust, opinionated outbox routing on every operation), the framework adds those layers above — it does not push them down into the protocol layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1436:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1775:+docs/aim.md:184:│   │                              # outbox routing. Pure Rust, no FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1437:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1777:+docs/aim.md:254:- Two pre-existing TypeScript Nostr libraries — intentionally unnamed here — supply the high-level application architecture (event store, models, actions, sessions, outbox routing, NIP-77 sync, wallet, messaging, web-of-trust, developer guardrails) being translated into Rust idiom under the RMP architectural skeleton.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1438:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1783:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1439:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1787:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1440:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1788:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1441:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1794:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1442:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1795:+docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1443:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1797:+docs/plan.md:30:- NIP-65 outbox routing (hardcoded content + indexer relays today).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4431:+- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1479:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4489:+You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4548:++| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4600:++**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1483:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5633:++## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1484:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5639:++- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5749:++### 2.2 Reuse in outbox routing (M2)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1486:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5862:+Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5900:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1500:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5902:+docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5922:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5923:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5924:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
