docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1533:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5964:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1534:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5965:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1535:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5966:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1536:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5972:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1537:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5973:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1538:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5974:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1539:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5976:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4410:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1540:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5977:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4412:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1541:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5978:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4420:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1542:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5981:+docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1543:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5986:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:15:You are reviewing merge fcf8b431b8d86f1801fef2fad26e81fbf56506f4 on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1618:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6080:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1685:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6224:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3912:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1686:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6225:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3913:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1759:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6299:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1760:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6300:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1761:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6301:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1787:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6364:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1788:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6386:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1789:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6416:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1790:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6417:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1791:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6425:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1812:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6473:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1813:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6475:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1814:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6476:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1815:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6477:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1816:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6478:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1820:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6503:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1821:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6504:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1822:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6505:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1848:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6546:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:612:+  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1856:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6578:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6582:+docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1924:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6726:+250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1925:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6727:+2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1927:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6729:+2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6752:+    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1929:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6762:+    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1930:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6775:+    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6781:+    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6794:+    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1933:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6798:+    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1934:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6872:+   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1935:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6933:+   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1938:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7164:+    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1939:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7174:+    30	- NIP-65 outbox routing (hardcoded content + indexer relays today).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1940:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7209:+    65	4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1941:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7210:+    66	5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7244:+   100	- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7262:+   118	### M2 — Subscription compilation + outbox routing
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1944:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7270:+   126	- `nmp-nip65` protocol module: Mailboxes view module (parsed kind:10002); outbox routing as a planner subsystem; recompilation triggers (kind:10002 arrival, view open, relay reconnect).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1945:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7346:+    23	| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1947:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7600:+A few likely consistency issues are emerging: key-layout drift between the master doc and `keys.md`, trait methods named in summaries but absent from the actual trait, and a possible D2/working-set leak around the “open view cover” being outside the trait contract. I’m checking those against the exact sub-doc lines now.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1949:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7885:+   282	## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)

All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2094:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2095:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2096:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2097:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2098:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2099:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2100:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:1:# M2 — Subscription compilation + outbox routing
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2101:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:11:- `nmp-nip65` protocol module: Mailboxes view module (parsed kind:10002); outbox routing as a planner subsystem; recompilation triggers (kind:10002 arrival, view open, relay reconnect).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2103:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:87:+- **M2 (subscription compilation + outbox routing + NIP-65):** design landed; 8 codex-flagged issues being addressed (T13).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2104:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:99:+- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2105:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:100:+  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2106:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:101:+  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2107:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:102:+  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:104:+  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2110:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:105:+  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2111:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:162:+| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2112:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:175:+2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2113:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:194:+> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2114:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:210:+- [Capabilities & rendering — best-effort placeholders, in-place refinement](framework-magic/capabilities.md) (C13)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2119:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:230:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2120:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:231:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2121:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:293:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:311:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:318:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2124:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2129:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2132:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2133:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2134:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2135:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2136:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2137:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2140:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2143:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1723:+Applesauce's outbox is not a relay-resolver service. It is a **reactive composition of three RxJS operators** over the EventStore. There is no central "outbox service" — there is just `OutboxModel = contacts() ⇒ includeMailboxes() ⇒ selectOptimalRelays()`. Every consumer that wants outbox routing subscribes to the model directly.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2179:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3646:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2180:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3647:README.md:88:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2181:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3648:README.md:101:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2182:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3649:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2183:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3650:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2184:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3651:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2185:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3652:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2186:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3653:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2187:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3654:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2188:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3655:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2189:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3656:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2190:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3657:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2191:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3658:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2192:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3659:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2193:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3660:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2194:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3661:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2195:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3662:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2196:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3663:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2197:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3664:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2198:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3665:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2199:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2200:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3667:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2201:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3668:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:68:+**`nostr-lmdb` gives us** (per `docs.rs/nostr-lmdb`): `save_event(&Event)`, `event_by_id(&EventId)`, `check_id(&EventId) -> DatabaseEventStatus`, `query(Filter) -> Events`, `count(Filter)`, `delete(Filter)`, `wipe()`, `negentropy_items(Filter) -> Vec<(EventId, Timestamp)>`. It owns the page allocator, the LMDB environment, primary by-id store, indexes derived from `Filter`, replaceable / parameterized-replaceable supersession, and NIP-09 delete handling. It is the only mature Rust LMDB store for Nostr events with proven NIP-77 integration; reinventing it is a year of work that we will not recoup.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:74:+| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:154:+  last_negentropy_state: Option<Vec<u8>>, // engine-opaque resume blob
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:213:+On duplicate-id insert (§7.1 row 2), `insert()` does not touch the primary; it only updates the provenance sidecar (`last_seen_ms` bump on the matching `ProvenanceEntry`, or append). The "primary relay" — for outbox-routing scoring (M2) and ADR-0007 diagnostics — is deterministically the first relay observed (`sources[0]` after sort by `first_seen_ms`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:959:+    pub last_negentropy_state: Option<Vec<u8>>,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1202:+Sub-db: `watermarks`. Populated by the M4 NIP-77 negentropy engine and consulted by the M2 subscription planner (per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 "coverage-aware backfill").
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1214:+    pub last_negentropy_state: Option<Vec<u8>>, // engine-opaque resume blob (M4)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1220:+`last_negentropy_state` is an opaque byte blob written by the NIP-77 engine (M4) — the store does not interpret it. Empty for `ReqScan` / `Manual` rows.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1244:+For installations with O(100k+) watermarks (an edge case — typical apps see O(100)–O(10k)), the in-memory map switches to a lazy-loaded variant that pages on demand. Threshold and switching logic deferred to M4 when the negentropy engine's real-world row count is measured.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1275:+### 2.2 Reuse in outbox routing (M2)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1426:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1428:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1429:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1431:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1432:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1433:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1434:docs/plan.md:100:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1438:docs/plan.md:271:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1439:docs/plan.md:335:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1441:docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1442:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1443:docs/plan.md:380:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity contract, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1449:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1450:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1460:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1739:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1461:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1743:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1744:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1465:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1754:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1466:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1467:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1756:docs/plan.md:100:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1470:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1774:docs/plan.md:271:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1471:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1776:docs/plan.md:335:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1472:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1777:docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1778:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1474:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1779:docs/plan.md:380:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1982:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2002:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1484:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2067:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2112:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1490:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1491:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1492:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1498:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1500:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1605:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2320:++**Code Quality:** Zero bugs. All 20 code inspection findings are acceptable or justified by design. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1607:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1608:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2397:++  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1609:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2404:++- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1649:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1650:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1674:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3418:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1675:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3423:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1676:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3424:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1677:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3426:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1678:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3427:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1750:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3912:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1751:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3913:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1752:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3914:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1821:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3985:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1824:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1825:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1826:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1855:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1856:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1868:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
