docs/design/framework-magic.md:37:| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:5:> **Status:** Draft. Lives in `crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/`. Gated as a Phase 9 deliverable in `plan.md`; capture-mode scenarios run as early as Phase 2.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:46:crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:67:nmp-testing firehose-bench live --scenario sustained_firehose \
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:70:    --storage lmdb --db-path /tmp/firehose-bench-db
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:84:nmp-testing firehose-bench replay --trace traces/2026-05-17-sustained-firehose.cap \
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:260:| Concern | reactivity-bench | firehose-bench |
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:279:The two are complementary. Reactivity-bench answers "does the algorithm scale?"; firehose-bench answers "does the system work under realistic conditions?"
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:289:Trace files live in `crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/traces/`. They are LFS-tracked (binary, multi-MB). A trace is invalidated when:
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:332:The findings get a write-up in `docs/perf/firehose-bench/<date>-run-NNN.md` and trigger ADRs for any design revisions, exactly the same pattern as reactivity-bench run 001.
docs/plan/principles.md:6:2. **Real measured evidence over modeled budgets.** Modeled passes in `firehose-bench` replay establish the budget contract. Real passes in `firehose-bench live` against the iOS / Android / Desktop / Web app are the actual gate.
docs/plan/principles.md:9:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the [M11](m11-podcast.md) podcast-app proof.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:1:# M11 — Podcast app (the `../podcast` rebuild on NMP — the kernel-boundary proof)
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:61:- `nmp-podcast` is **not a v1 deliverable** unless a real published NIP is selected during M11 design (e.g. NIP-54 or a successor) and the choice is recorded in `docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md`. Until then, the podcast app uses RSS + Podcast 2.0 namespaces (chapters, transcripts, value-for-value) via `podcast-feeds`, and Nostr is the **social overlay only** — kind:1 discussion threads referencing the episode URL/GUID, kind:7 reactions, NIP-57 zaps. The decision is locked in M11 step-0; no `NIP-XX` placeholders allowed in code or in plans past that point.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:72:- **The capability families added in M11 are general** (audio playback, background work, local notifications, HTTP, embedding, KV-store). Their request/response shapes are not podcast-specific.
docs/plan/m11-podcast.md:95:- Battery drain during 1 hour of background playback ≤ Swift baseline + 10 %.
docs/plan/status.md:13:- **Benches** in `crates/nmp-testing`: `reactivity-bench` (composite-key reverse index + coalescer + working set; run 002 passed all ADR-0001..0004 gates) and `firehose-bench` (replay + capture + live modes; replay scenarios pass the modeled budget contract).
docs/plan/status.md:14:- **Perf reports** in `docs/perf/` documenting reactivity-bench run 002, firehose-bench replay runs, and three iOS measurement reports (relay lifecycle, profile/thread subscriptions, the primal slice baseline).
docs/plan/status.md:42:- The plan didn't reflect that M0 and M1 are largely done.
docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/plan/status.md:44:- **M11 was generic.** This rewrite ties it concretely to `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (the fully-functional Swift app) as the rebuild target, with copy-first UI fidelity and an explicit view-by-view module mapping.
docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:1:# Design: `../podcast` rebuild on NMP (M11)
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:3:> **Status:** Proposed (M11 prep)
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:5:> **Scope:** How M11 rebuilds `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` on NMP — kernel-boundary proof + pixel-perfect UI parity.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:15:**This is M11's load-bearing kernel-boundary check.** If the kernel needs even one podcast noun to make it work, the boundary is wrong and we go back to fix it. The exit gate is dual: (a) `nmp-core` gains zero podcast nouns, verified by grep + manual review; (b) screenshot diff vs `../podcast` is ≤ 1 px on every screen, font-rendering exceptions whitelisted.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:17:**Scope clarification (additive vs strict parity).** [`docs/plan/m11-podcast.md`](../plan/m11-podcast.md) lists `ImportOpml` as an action. The reference Swift app has no OPML import (`AddPodcastView` is a single-URL form, `LibraryView` lists subscribed podcasts only). M11 ships `ImportOpml` as **a Rust-only `ActionModule` with no UI entry point** — no button, no new view, no change to any copied Swift file. Adding a UI element for OPML import to any `Views/` file would violate the pixel-parity gate (see [`copy.md`](podcast/copy.md) §Invariants). The action is callable only via the kernel's external API (e.g., a CLI or post-M11 Settings entry); it is deferred to post-M11 for any UI surface. All other M11 features map 1:1 to an existing Swift surface.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:19:**Multiplatform-ready.** Although M11 ships only the iPhone target, every extension crate is pure Rust and compiles to wasm32 + android targets (capabilities aside). Android/Desktop/Web shells are explicitly post-M11; the design must not foreclose them.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:36:| 8 | [`podcast/exit-gate.md`](podcast/exit-gate.md) | Step 8: M11 exit-gate-to-evidence-artifact map. |
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:124:1. [`copy.md`](podcast/copy.md) — Step 0: `cp -R`, commit, screenshot baseline.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:131:8. [`exit-gate.md`](podcast/exit-gate.md) — verify against M11's exit-gate bullets.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:133:Each step ends with: workspace `cargo test --workspace` green; the per-step screenshot diffs pass; a perf bullet from §M11 measured numbers.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:152:## 8. Out of scope for M11
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:154:- Android / Desktop / Web targets — post-M14 (UniFFI migration) and M15 (cross-platform).
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:155:- Nostr social overlay (NIP-XX podcast events, V4V zaps, comment threads). Listed in [`docs/plan/m11-podcast.md`](../plan/m11-podcast.md) as opportunistic; deferred to a `nmp-podcast` Nostr protocol module post-M11.

docs/plan/post-v1.md:5:These milestones were deferred from the v1 ladder per [scope adjustments 2026-05-18](scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md). They are not dropped — they are sequenced after [M17](m17-release.md).
docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/plan/post-v1.md:11:## Post-v1 M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:1:# M10.5 — FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof *(hard gate before M11 starts)*
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:3:> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Hard gate between Arc 1 (social stack) and the kernel-boundary proof in [M11](m11-podcast.md).
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:5:**Demo product:** The iOS Twitter slice from [M1](m1-twitter-slice.md)–[M10](m10-blossom.md) subjected to a published, exhaustive stress harness on the iOS simulator and a real iPhone 12. The kernel↔FFI↔SwiftUI path is proven, in measured numbers, to be **rock-solid and demonstrably performant** before a single line of the podcast app is written.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:19:- **Real-device measurement on iPhone 12** (one full battery of `firehose-bench live` against primal, all 8 scenarios from `docs/design/firehose-bench.md` §3); produces `docs/perf/m10.5/iphone12-baseline.md` with hardware-tagged numbers.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:20:- **Simulator-driven UI test fleet** (parallel Sonnet agents via the `mcp__xcode` and `BrowserAgent`/`QATester` skills) exercising the app from the outside — boot sim, launch app, tap, scroll, swipe, kill-relaunch — capturing screenshots and assertions per scripted scenario. Every M1–M10 user-visible feature gets a UI test; failures block the milestone.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:23:- **All M1–M10 perf reports re-run** on the final FFI surface to confirm no regressions.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:38:- All M1–M10 perf reports re-run cleanly on the post-M10.5 binaries; no regression > 5 % on any p99 number.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:1:# Design: Subscription Compilation + Outbox Routing (M2)
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:3:> **Status:** Draft (M2 design + impl-prep).
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:6:> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:30:| 9 | M2 exit-gate audit test path + assertions | tests.md |
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:39:3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:9:| Cross-FFI | UniFFI binding round-trip tests | Bindings stability, rev ordering, callback delivery | `apps/<name>/nmp-app-<name>/tests/` (post-M14) |
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:12:| Firehose bench (modeled) | `firehose-bench replay --standard --fail-on-gate` | Budget contract for the runtime | `crates/nmp-testing/bin/firehose-bench/` |
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:13:| Firehose bench (live) | `firehose-bench live` against the real iOS app | Runtime evidence end-to-end | reports in `docs/perf/m<N>/` |
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:17:The cross-platform consistency tests are the highest-value tier post-[M15](m15-cross-platform.md).
docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:1:# M2 — Subscription compilation + outbox routing
docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:5:**Demo product:** Same iOS app as [M1](m1-twitter-slice.md), but timeline subscriptions are routed per-author to those authors' write relays per NIP-65, not to the hardcoded primal/purplepag.es pair. Diagnostics screen visibly shows the per-relay fan-out and which authors each relay covers.
docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:13:- Per-pubkey relay-list cache (durable, even before LMDB lands — keep it in-memory until [M3](m3-persistence.md), but the data model is correct).
docs/plan/m10-blossom.md:1:# M10 — Blossom + media + long-running capabilities
docs/plan/m10-blossom.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec §7.11. Establishes the **long-running capability lifecycle pattern** that the podcast app ([M11](m11-podcast.md)) builds on:
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:10:| **M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)** | **Deferred to post-v1.** Wallet is huge surface area (NWC, NIP-57, NIP-60, NIP-61) and not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:11:| *(none)* | **New M11.5 — Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate.** Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app proves the boundary. Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs; the rebuild port it onto NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern). Adds NIP-29 as a first-class protocol module crate (groups, moderation, joining flow, kind 39000–39003 metadata events) done in NMP-idiomatic shape. |
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:17:### Folded into M2 (subscription compilation + outbox routing)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:20:- **NDK reference:** how NDK auto-follows kind:3 changes and re-routes its open subs. (Captured in M2 research wave; agents fan out.)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:29:### New M1.5 (or fold into M2 if size allows) — "Framework magic" contract
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:33:- Kind:3 auto-tracking → subscription recompile (M2 implementation)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:35:- Outbox routing on every read/write (M2)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:36:- Subscription dedup + merge + auto-close (M2)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:37:- Provenance preserved across redeliveries (M3)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:38:- DM-private events refuse non-inbox relays (M9 — deferred but the structural ban stays in M2 outbox planner)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:56:When all four land, a plan-revisor agent folds the findings into `docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`, `docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md`, the new `docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md`, the new `docs/plan/framework-magic.md`, and removes M9 + M12 from the active ladder (moving them to a `docs/plan/post-v1.md` companion).
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:60:- DMs deferred ≠ no privacy at v1. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays (M2) stays. When DMs land post-v1, they slot in cleanly because the routing contract is already there.
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:62:- These deferrals shrink v1 surface area and let M10.5 + the two app-rebuilds (podcast + highlighter) be the load-bearing kernel-boundary proofs they need to be.
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:68:M1    Twitter slice on iOS                                            🟡 hardening

docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:79:M11   ../podcast rebuild on NMP                                       — design done
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:80:M11.5 ../hl/app (Highlighter) rebuild + nmp-nip29 crate               ← NEW

docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:83:M14   UniFFI migration                                                — pending
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:84:M15   Cross-platform (Android + Desktop + Web)                        — pending
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:85:M16   CLI + starter app + recipes                                     — pending
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:86:M17   v1 release                                                      — pending
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:89:Plus the cross-cutting "framework magic" contract that grows across M2 + M6 + M8 with its own dedicated test suite.
docs/plan/decision-log.md:22:Every design doc has measurable gates. Gates run on the reactivity-bench harness (or `firehose-bench` for end-to-end behavior). Failures revise the design **before** implementation. Pre-implementation measurement is cheaper than post-implementation rework. Run 001 of reactivity-bench established the pattern: the reverse-index direction was validated (100×–1000× headroom), one design refinement landed (composite keys), and two budget bugs surfaced (per-view delta, working-set memory) — all before any view-kind code shipped.
docs/plan/decision-log.md:31:Each milestone moves the boundary rightward — replaces another modeled segment with a real adapter and graduates the corresponding firehose-bench scenarios from `modeled` to `measured` in `docs/perf/`.
docs/plan/m17-release.md:1:# M17 — v1 release
docs/plan/status.md:13:- **Benches** in `crates/nmp-testing`: `reactivity-bench` (composite-key reverse index + coalescer + working set; run 002 passed all ADR-0001..0004 gates) and `firehose-bench` (replay + capture + live modes; replay scenarios pass the modeled budget contract).
docs/plan/status.md:14:- **Perf reports** in `docs/perf/` documenting reactivity-bench run 002, firehose-bench replay runs, and three iOS measurement reports (relay lifecycle, profile/thread subscriptions, the primal slice baseline).
docs/plan/status.md:42:- The plan didn't reflect that M0 and M1 are largely done.
docs/plan/status.md:43:- **No dedicated FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof gate before the kernel-boundary proof.** The prior M11 implicitly assumed the FFI surface was ready; this rewrite makes it a separate milestone (M10.5).
docs/plan/status.md:44:- **M11 was generic.** This rewrite ties it concretely to `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (the fully-functional Swift app) as the rebuild target, with copy-first UI fidelity and an explicit view-by-view module mapping.
docs/plan/status.md:46:The plan below is a single ladder of eighteen milestones (M0–M17, with M10.5 inserted as the FFI gate), each producing a runnable artifact, ordered so that each milestone strictly adds capabilities to the prior demoable product.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:7:- **[M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) (outbox), [M3](m3-persistence.md) (LMDB), [M4](m4-negentropy.md) (negentropy)** can pipeline tightly: M3 + M4 are almost mechanically pluggable once M2's compiled-plan abstraction exists.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:8:- **[M5](m5-nip42.md) (NIP-42)** is independent of M3/M4 and can be done alongside.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:9:- **[M6](m6-signers-write.md) (signer + write path) is a serialization point** — most downstream milestones ([M7](m7-interaction-loop.md), [M8](m8-multi-account.md), [M9](m9-messaging.md), [M10](m10-blossom.md), [M12](m12-wallet.md)) depend on it. Land this fast.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:10:- **[M10.5](m10.5-ffi-hardening.md) (FFI hardening)** is itself parallelizable: the stress harness, the iPhone-12 perf rerun, the UI-script Sonnet-agent fleet, and the FFI surface audit are four independent workstreams.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:11:- **[M11](m11-podcast.md) (podcast app)** starts only after M10.5 passes. Its own internal parallelism is wide: the copy step + each `*-core` Rust extension crate + each view-wiring batch can be split across agents (one per view group: Library, Feed, Player, Insights, Ask, Settings, Components, plus one agent per LLM/RAG/feeds module).
docs/plan/parallelization.md:12:- **[M15](m15-cross-platform.md) (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once [M14](m14-uniffi.md) (UniFFI) lands.
docs/plan/parallelization.md:14:A team of two could run M5 alongside the M2–M4 sequence with no integration risk. With parallel-agent execution (this session's mode), the practical limit is conflict surface: independent crates, independent docs, and independent platform shells fan out cleanly; shared mutable files (e.g. `nmp.toml`, the codegen output, `Cargo.toml`) serialize.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:1:# M14 — UniFFI migration
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:1:# Design: LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy (M3)
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:3:> **Audience:** kernel implementers landing M3 (persistence).
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:23:| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:31:| `nmp dump` deterministic export | Out of scope | M3 exit criteria; M11 cross-app proof |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:33:**Therefore.** `EventStore` is a NMP-owned trait, with one production impl `LmdbEventStore` that holds (a) a `NostrLMDB` for the canonical event store and Nostr-shaped queries, and (b) NMP-owned secondary LMDB sub-databases under the same `lmdb::Environment` for the gap rows. The in-memory backend (`MemEventStore`) remains, both for tests and as the web-pre-M15 fallback. See [`lmdb/trait.md`](lmdb/trait.md) for the exact trait shape and the relayed-vs-owned method split.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:37:- *Use `NostrLMDB` directly, no wrapper.* Loses every gap row above. Forces the kernel actor to know about LMDB transactions and a non-NMP concrete type, breaking the `Box<dyn EventStore>` substitutability M3 requires.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:39:- *SQLite-backed `nostr-sdk` store.* Larger memory footprint at our 10k-event hot working set; iOS-disk-WAL fsync cost not justified for this access pattern. Held in reserve for the web port (M15) if IndexedDB OPFS proves unworkable.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:109:Key: `filter_hash[32] || relay_url_bytes` (no length prefix needed — relay URL is the variable suffix; lookup uses exact key). Populated by M4 (NIP-77) and consulted by M2's planner (cache-coverage check before issuing backfill REQ). Survives restarts; loaded into the actor on startup as a `HashMap<(filter_hash, relay_url), WatermarkRow>` for hot lookups, with all writes going through `EventStore` for durability.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:115:The current `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41`) discards the concrete `M: DomainModule` type after `register_domain::<M>()` returns — only the `ModuleDescriptor` is retained. The store cannot get from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()` at runtime. M3 adds a `DomainFactories { schema_version: fn() -> u32, migrations: fn() -> Vec<DomainMigration>, indexes: fn() -> Vec<DomainIndex> }` struct attached per descriptor, populated by capturing the `M::*` consts and fns in `fn`-pointer closures at register time. This matches the existing `key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` pattern in `DomainIndex` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:18`) — no `Box<dyn DomainModule>` and no new trait object-safety constraints on `DomainModule`. The change is additive to the substrate module surface. See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4.1 for the registry-side code shape.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:142:ADR-0003's numbers are preserved as the M3 exit gate (§11 below): ≤ 100 MB working-set at 100 active views / 10k hot events / 1M cached on disk.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:162:On duplicate-id insert (§7.1 row 2), `insert()` does not touch the primary; it only updates the provenance sidecar (`last_seen_ms` bump on the matching `ProvenanceEntry`, or append). The "primary relay" — for outbox-routing scoring (M2) and ADR-0007 diagnostics — is deterministically the first relay observed (`sources[0]` after sort by `first_seen_ms`).
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:206:| Cold-start time-to-first-painted-timeline on iPhone 12 (primed LMDB, last session's events on disk) | ≤ 1.5 s p99 | `firehose-bench live cold_start --device iphone12` |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:214:Each gate is measurable; any miss revises the design via an ADR before M3 is declared complete (per `plan.md` §1.6 "no silent endings").
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:220:3. **Projection cache durability.** Currently in-memory in the existing kernel (`kernel/mod.rs:293` `profiles: HashMap`). Do we persist projection caches as a `DomainModule` or rebuild from events at cold-start? Rebuild is simpler and avoids cache-staleness bugs but adds startup cost; persistence is faster but requires invalidation logic on kind:0 replacement. Recommended default: rebuild on cold-start, measure, decide whether to add the persistence layer in M3.x or M4.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:222:5. **iOS keychain-stored encryption-at-rest key for LMDB.** Out of scope for M3 (mentioned for M6 keychain work) but the schema must not assume cleartext-on-disk forever; reserve a `meta` row for `encryption_version: u32` so a future migration can wrap pages.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:223:6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:234:The M3 implementation deletes none of the existing files outright — the kernel's `events: HashMap` and `profiles: HashMap` are replaced by calls to `Box<dyn EventStore>` held inside the `Kernel` struct, and the per-field tests (`kernel/tests.rs`) shift to the new trait via `MemEventStore`. No public FFI surface changes.

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:10:| 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

User landed major scope shift: M9 DMs + M12 Wallet → post-v1; M11.5 Highlighter rebuild + nmp-nip29 crate added; framework-magic contract (kind:3 auto-tracking, bunker://, new-nsec, NDK/Applesauce-style "just works") folded across M2/M6/M8. T8 codex-fixer-1 + T11 codex-fixer-2 both finished (commits fb139ab, f68a479, b4b6afa, 151c80c, d871767, 80217cc — five sequential codex-driven fix cycles). T8 worktree cleaned (acdef1454b384c4b2). DISK PRESSURE: master Volumes/Data hit 96%; purged 6 stale `Podcastr-*` DerivedData dirs from prior podcast-rmp sessions (recovered ~7GB); m1-hardener notified to use `-derivedDataPath` going forward. T12 (clippy clean) dispatched (clippy-cleaner). User asleep — autonomous mode armed (cron f7c9fecf). |
docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/aim.md:60:The bible's reference crate layout — `rust/` for the core (cdylib + staticlib + rlib), `uniffi-bindgen/` for the binding generator, `ios/`, `android/`, `crates/<app>-desktop/`, a `justfile` for build orchestration, an optional Nix flake — is the layout this framework will scaffold for users. We adopt RMP's `rmp-cli` as the model for our own scaffolding tool.
docs/aim.md:187:│   │                              # uniffi::Record/Enum derives on state types.
docs/aim.md:188:│   ├── <framework>-wasm         # wasm-bindgen wrapper for web/Node/RN.
docs/aim.md:205:│   └── typescript/              # Generated wasm-bindgen TS, checked in.
docs/aim.md:215:The core crate compiles as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`. Desktop and CLI consumers link the rlib directly (no FFI). iOS links the staticlib via xcframework. Android links the cdylib via cargo-ndk. Web compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown via the wasm crate. **One source of truth, four delivery paths.**
docs/plan.md:49:| [M14](plan/m14-uniffi.md) | UniFFI migration | Arc 3 — Release | |
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:25:│  Desktop iced shell (no FFI; direct rlib link)               │
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:69:- **FFI.** Desktop slice uses direct rlib linking (per spec §4.3.1, this is bible-canonical for desktop). UniFFI is wired in when porting the slice to iOS/Android.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:119:- **Slice through FFI to iOS first.** Rejected — desktop's direct rlib linking lets us validate the architecture without UniFFI noise. iOS wrap comes after the actor's API is stable.
crates/nmp-core/Cargo.toml:9:crate-type = ["staticlib", "rlib"]
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:12:The framework's existing FFI surface (UniFFI on iOS/Android, wasm-bindgen on web) expects **concrete, closed types**. Open enums don't exist in UniFFI. Two paths are possible:
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:53:│   ├── lib.rs             # uniffi::setup_scaffolding!()
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:60:├── uniffi.toml
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:72:#[derive(Clone, uniffi::Enum)]
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:132:A Swift app links `NmpAppTwitter.xcframework`. It sees Swift types `AppAction.kernel(...)`, `AppAction.nip01(...)`, `AppAction.twitter(...)`. The generated wrappers (`useTimeline`, `useProfile`, `@Twitter` property wrapper, etc.) come from the same codegen step.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:186:- Phase 1a.3 (iOS port) consumes the same generated crate via xcframework. iOS Swift code sees typed enums.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:35:├── web/                           # vite + wasm-bindgen shell
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:56:Checks: Rust toolchain version, cross-compilation targets installed, Xcode (macOS only), Android SDK + NDK, JDK 17, `cargo-ndk`, `xcodegen`, `just`, `node`, `wasm-pack`. Reports per-platform readiness and prints exact install commands for what is missing.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:64:- `cdylib + staticlib + rlib` from generated `nmp-app-<name>`.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:65:- iOS: cross-compile, build `Nmp.xcframework`, link as non-embedded static framework via XcodeGen.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:66:- Android: `cargo-ndk` produces per-ABI `.so` into `jniLibs/`; JNA loads at runtime.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:168:- **Action.** A `uniffi::Enum` variant + an async fn in `nmp-actions` that implements it. Fire-and-forget at the FFI boundary.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:170:- **AppState.** A `uniffi::Record` carrying everything the UI needs to render. Cloned across FFI.
docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:173:- **Capability.** A native-implemented Rust trait (`#[uniffi::export(callback_interface)]`) that lets Rust ask the OS to do something. Bounded; policy-free.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:41:| Reference platform | Desktop iced (rlib-linked, no FFI) | Maintained alongside iOS as a UniFFI-free diagnostic target — if something breaks on iOS but works on desktop, it's an FFI/Xcode issue, not architecture. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:76:- `nmp-ffi` crate with `FfiApp` (`uniffi::Object`), `AppReconciler` callback interface, generated Swift bindings checked into `bindings/swift/`.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:78:- xcframework build pipeline in `justfile`: `just ios-rust`, `just ios-gen-swift`, `just ios-xcframework`, `just run-ios`.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:156:The desktop iced binary built in 1a.1 stays alive throughout 1a.2 through 1a.6. It links the rlib directly (no UniFFI). It is not a shipping product, but it acts as a non-FFI reference:
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:169:- **iOS toolchain risk is front-loaded.** UniFFI surprises, xcframework build, Xcode versioning — all happen in 1a.2. If they don't resolve cleanly, we know early and the desktop reference target is the fallback validation path.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:200:#[derive(Clone, uniffi::Record)]
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:207:#[derive(Clone, uniffi::Record)]
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:306:`WalletState` is a `uniffi::Record` field of `AppState`. Wallet attachment is an action; payment is an action; receipt verification is automatic. No wallet UI hook mutates proof, token, nutzap, or transaction state directly; those records live in the actor-owned domain store and transition through the action ledger.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:356:#[uniffi::export]
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:363:#[derive(uniffi::Record)]

docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1025:d660735:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:170:- **AppState.** A `uniffi::Record` carrying everything the UI needs to render. Cloned across FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1067:d660735:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:306:`WalletState` is a `uniffi::Record` field of `AppState`. Wallet attachment is an action; payment is an action; receipt verification is automatic. No wallet UI hook mutates proof, token, nutzap, or transaction state directly; those records live in the actor-owned domain store and transition through the action ledger.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7567:+   215	The core crate compiles as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`. Desktop and CLI consumers link the rlib directly (no FFI). iOS links the staticlib via xcframework. Android links the cdylib via cargo-ndk. docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:6068:    60	The bible's reference crate layout — `rust/` for the core (cdylib + staticlib + rlib), `uniffi-bindgen/` for the binding generator, `ios/`, `android/`, `crates/<app>-desktop/`, a `justfile` for build orchestration, an optional Nix flake — is the layout this framework will scaffold for users. We adopt RMP's `rmp-cli` as the model for our own scaffolding tool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:6089:docs/aim.md:60:The bible's reference crate layout — `rust/` for the core (cdylib + staticlib + rlib), `uniffi-bindgen/` for the binding generator, `ios/`, `android/`, `crates/<app>-desktop/`, a `justfile` for build orchestration, an optional Nix flake — is the layout this framework will scaffold for users. We adopt RMP's `rmp-cli` as the model for our own scaffolding tool.

 succeeded in 0ms:
    55	
    56	Checks: Rust toolchain version, cross-compilation targets installed, Xcode (macOS only), Android SDK + NDK, JDK 17, `cargo-ndk`, `xcodegen`, `just`, `node`, `wasm-pack`. Reports per-platform readiness and prints exact install commands for what is missing.
    57	
    58	---
    59	
    60	## 9. Build & toolchain
    61	
    62	The bible's pipeline is adopted verbatim:
    63	
    64	- `cdylib + staticlib + rlib` from generated `nmp-app-<name>`.
    65	- iOS: cross-compile, build `Nmp.xcframework`, link as non-embedded static framework via XcodeGen.
    66	- Android: `cargo-ndk` produces per-ABI `.so` into `jniLibs/`; JNA loads at runtime.
    67	- Desktop: direct path dependency; no FFI.
    68	- Web: `wasm-pack build` with `nmp-wasm`; output consumed by a vite shell.
    69	- `just` is the entry point for every build, gen, and run.
    70	- A Nix flake provisions the entire toolchain.

README.md:5:**Live status snapshot — see `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` for per-heartbeat detail and `docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md` for autonomous-mode decisions awaiting review.**
README.md:78:- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
README.md:94:| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
README.md:111:*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:3:Durable trail of the parallel-agent orchestration. One line per heartbeat or significant event.
docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:7:| 2026-05-18 01:24 | 0 | Session start. Pulled to e9cbafa. Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:12:- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:80:### No background-refresh API
docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:82:There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/research/ndk/kind3-auto-tracking.md:100:Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).

docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:62:`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).

docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/design/firehose-bench.md:92:**Replay is what CI runs.** Capture is run periodically (weekly?) to refresh traces; live is for manual investigation.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:128:- Pull-to-refresh: forces planner re-fetch on the active timeline.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:13:├── README.md
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:19:NDK also models an important operational truth: relay metadata can arrive late. A subscription may start with incomplete routing information, then improve when relay lists are discovered. The system should be able to refresh or expand active work when better metadata appears instead of requiring the app to tear down and recreate views.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:68:Relay capability should likewise be durable and observable. NDK's capability caching and Applesauce's explicit support checks both point to the same lesson: probing support on every operation wastes time, but assuming support forever is brittle. NMP should remember relay capabilities, refresh them deliberately, and expose degraded paths in diagnostics.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:126:- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:73:The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:44:+**Live status snapshot — see `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` for per-heartbeat detail and `docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md` for autonomous-mode decisions awaiting review.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:115:+- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:131:+| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:148:+*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:150:Review: (1) is the README accurate and consistent with what's in the repo, (2) any claims that contradict the doctrine or plan, (3) file size (README itself), (4) any TODO/FIXME. Be terse.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:189:README.md:13:- **M2 (subscription compilation + outbox routing + NIP-65):** design landed; 8 codex-flagged issues being addressed (T13).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:190:README.md:15:- **M4 (NIP-77 negentropy sync), M5 (NIP-42 auth), M6 (sessions + signers + write), M7 (interaction loop), M8 (multi-session):** designs pending after M2/M3 hardens.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:191:README.md:18:- **M11 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` on NMP):** design landed; pixel-parity UI copy + Rust-backed business logic + `rig.rs` LLM + RAG + podcast-feeds; awaits M10.5 empirical pass before impl starts.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:192:README.md:19:- **M11.5 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` Highlighter on NMP + `nmp-nip29` crate):** designed in flight (T18).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:193:README.md:21:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` URL onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed ([docs/design/framework-magic.md](docs/design/framework-magic.md)) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:194:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:195:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:196:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:197:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:198:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:199:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:200:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:201:README.md:36:- **DMs (was M9) and Wallet (was M12) deferred to post-v1.** Documented in `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`. M11.5 Highlighter takes their slot.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:202:README.md:38:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking is framework-magic** — when the active account's follow list changes, every open subscription that depends on "current user's follows" auto-recompiles on the wire. Apps dispatch zero code. The Applesauce/NDK research (`docs/research/`) confirmed core NDK does NOT provide this automatically — NMP must build it as framework code.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:203:README.md:40:- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:212:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:7:| 2026-05-18 01:24 | 0 | Session start. Pulled to e9cbafa. Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:231:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:237:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:269:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:309:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:545:* | | 51120cb perf(orchestration): HB4 (autonomous) — framework-magic landed + research + README
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:404:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:648:+- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1176:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:591:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2302:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:894:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:288:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:150:Review: (1) is the README accurate and consistent with what's in the repo, (2) any claims that contradict the doctrine or plan, (3) file size (README itself), (4) any TODO/FIXME. Be terse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1631:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:12:- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1753:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:515:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:66:Conditional GET (ETag / If-Modified-Since) for `RefreshFeed`: the kernel persists a `FeedFetchCache { feed_url, etag?, last_modified?, fetched_at_ms }` row per podcast. On refresh: send the headers; on 304 → emit `RefreshFeedOutput::NotModified`.

Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4924:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1042:+Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1117:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1118:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1124:+`refreshRelayConnections` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1127:+public refreshRelayConnections(): void {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1146:+- **Bypassed when `relaySet` is explicit** — if your subscription was opened with a fixed `relaySet`, no auto-refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1157:+| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1176:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1244:+### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1246:+There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1428:+## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1437:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1438:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1444:+`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2302:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

Bounded views | View registry, open/close/refresh actions, `ViewBatch`, claim GC, warmth TTL, raw/event-detail/timeline-like test views | Snapshots remain bounded by open views; shared materializations and empty payloads are deterministic |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:781:d660735:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:874:d660735:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:68:Relay capability should likewise be durable and observable. NDK's capability caching and Applesauce's explicit support checks both point to the same lesson: probing support on every operation wastes time, but assuming support forever is brittle. NMP should remember relay capabilities, refresh them deliberately, and expose degraded paths in diagnostics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1017:d660735:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:116:| 2. Bounded views | View registry, open/close/refresh actions, `ViewBatch`, claim GC, warmth TTL, raw/event-detail/timeline-like test views | Snapshots remain bounded by open views; shared materializations and empty payloads are deterministic |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:246:-- **1a.7** Profile screen + diagnostics + polish + second fixture — author-tap → profile screen with author-filtered Timeline; pagination; ADR-0007 diagnostics screen; second non-Nostr fixture module (small notes app) demonstrating boundary breadth; pull-to-refresh; error states. ~1–2 weeks.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:453:-- Cached-data-never-withheld: any kind:0 in store is served immediately by profile view; background refresh does not gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:530:+- `BackgroundWorkCapability`: kernel registers periodic background tasks (feed refresh, scheduled downloads); platform implements via BGTask scheduler (iOS) / WorkManager (Android).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:672:-The `firehose-bench` harness (per `docs/design/firehose-bench.md`) is the load-bearing tool here. It runs in three modes: **live** (real relays, real network), **capture** (records live to a trace), **replay** (deterministic re-execution against `MockRelay`). Replay is what CI uses; live + capture are for soak testing and trace refresh.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:508:++- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1514:++This assertion is what `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §2 line 19 calls out as NDK's important operational truth: "metadata can arrive late… the system should be able to refresh or expand active work without the app tearing down and recreating views."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1691:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:126:- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1774:+docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2091:+docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:78:    kinds: vec![0, 10002],                      // kind:0 (profile) + mailbox-tagged refresh

This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:218:++- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:234:++| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:251:++*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:330:++The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:504:++The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1323:+@@ -8,4 +8,5 @@ Durable trail of the parallel-agent orchestration. One line per heartbeat or sig
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1329:++| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1932:++- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2326:++Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2402:++            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2408:++`refreshRelayConnections` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2411:++public refreshRelayConnections(): void {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2430:++- **Bypassed when `relaySet` is explicit** — if your subscription was opened with a fixed `relaySet`, no auto-refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2441:++| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2460:++- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2528:++### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2530:++There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2712:++## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2721:++        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2722:++            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2728:++`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4035:+    19	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7235:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7236:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7237:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7238:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7239:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7240:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7241:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7242:README.md:33:  Three additional load-bearing rules are not D-numbered but are equally binding (RMP-bible invariants from [`docs/aim.md` §2](docs/aim.md)): **errors never cross FFI** (operational failures become `toast: Option<String>` state fields), **capabilities report; never decide policy** (native bridges execute and report; Rust decides retry/recovery), and the **reactivity contract** (composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7243:README.md:90:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:464:+- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:595:+    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:632:+    /// mailbox refresh).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1470:+This assertion is what `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §2 line 19 calls out as NDK's important operational truth: "metadata can arrive late… the system should be able to refresh or expand active work without the app tearing down and recreating views."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1647:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:126:- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1730:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:122:+The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:296:+The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1486:    73	The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:58:+- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:327:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:19:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:405:docs/design/framework-magic.md:73:The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:410:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:16:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking**: when a kind:3 (follow list) lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows" — recompiling transparently without the app re-issuing the REQ. Reference: NDK auto-follow kind:3 changes + Applesauce event-store query builder auto-tracking.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:537:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:70:The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:592:    19	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1050:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3641:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1072:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4048:README.md:35:- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)
