Covers: framework-magic contract (edc17b0), m11 followup cleanups (8302cc1/59f8d1b/fd8f173/2477372), autonomous-mode log (fa7d144), applesauce+ndk research notes (8d633e8), top-level README.md (810d0f8), HB4 orchestration log (51120cb).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:29: README.md                                          | 109 +++++++++++
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:61:51120cb perf(orchestration): HB4 (autonomous) — framework-magic landed + research + README

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:79:+**Live status snapshot — see `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` for per-heartbeat detail and `docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md` for autonomous-mode decisions awaiting review.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:150:+- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:166:+| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:183:+*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:262:+The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:436:+The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1255:@@ -8,4 +8,5 @@ Durable trail of the parallel-agent orchestration. One line per heartbeat or sig
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1261:+| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1864:+- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2258:+Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2334:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2373:+| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2392:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2460:+### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2462:+There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2644:+## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2653:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2654:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2660:+`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3496:    76	- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3640:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3641:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3642:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3643:README.md:28:  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3644:README.md:29:  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3645:README.md:30:  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3646:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3967:    19	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4048:README.md:35:- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4132:   129	Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

Findings:

- `810d0f8:README.md:28-31` mislabels doctrine. It says D2 = reactivity, D3 = errors-never-FFI, D5 = capabilities-report, but canonical §1.5 says D2 = negentropy, D3 = outbox, D5 = bounded snapshots in [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54). This contradicts the doctrine and plan. Current HEAD appears to have a later fix in `3f5a2b3`.

- [README.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/README.md:21) claims `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` exists with 14 named tests. It does not exist in `810d0f8`, and `crates/nmp-testing/tests/` does not exist. Either add the skeletal ignored test file or downgrade the wording to planned tests.

- `810d0f8:README.md:72` says the kernel is compiled as `cdylib + staticlib + rlib`, but [crates/nmp-core/Cargo.toml](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/crates/nmp-core/Cargo.toml:9) only declares `staticlib` + `rlib`. Present-tense README claim is inaccurate.

File size: OK, `810d0f8:README.md` is 109 LOC, under 300.
